design_philosophy.core_loop: Fire bubbles upward to match three or more same-colored bubbles; clear all bubbles before ceiling descends to bottom line; progress through increasingly complex level layouts
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Time pressure via descending ceiling that accelerates as player approaches failure state; tension between random bubble generation and strategic aiming
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Precise bubble-matching mastery; satisfying chain reactions from well-placed shots; accessible puzzle-solving progression
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberately simple, single-mechanic design focused on addictive tile-matching loop rather than complexity; random bubble color selection from remaining colors creates constraints; ceiling drop mechanic creates inherent time pressure without explicit timer

difficulty.continues_system: Game over when bubbles cross bottom line; restart current level
difficulty.checkpointing: Level-based progression; each cleared level advances to next pattern
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player can control bubble aim and fire timing; arcade version has 30 levels with presumably escalating complexity; no difficulty selection documented

progression.unlock_mechanism: Level completion unlocks next arena pattern; arcade version features 30 sequential levels
replayability_hooks: Two-player competitive mode for head-to-head matching
Pattern memorization for optimal shot placement

world_building_techniques: Bubble Bobble IP licensing provides character/thematic context but no story integration

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Bust-A-Move SNES)
